Description: Design and development of dashboards that provide visibility into system health, integration status, and operational performance across simulated systems. This includes defining key metrics, status indicators, and visual representations that enable users to monitor system behavior and identify issues.

Simulation of data exchange between multiple systems to replicate real-world integration workflows. This includes generating synthetic data, modeling system interactions, and representing how data flows across distributed environments.

Implementation of structured message logging to simulate healthcare-style data exchanges using HL7-like formats. This includes capturing, storing, and displaying message events to provide traceability of system interactions and support observability.

Design of the platform’s data architecture, including relational schema definition, data relationships, and handling of normalized and denormalized structures. Ensures data integrity, consistency across system components, and scalability to support simulated multi-system environments.

Summary: HL7-style message log simulation and visualization
Description:
Acceptance Criteria:

* Message logs capture events with timestamps, statuses, and are viewable in UI

Summary: Error state simulation for system failures and delays
Description:
Acceptance Criteria:

* System displays simulated failure states and reflects them in monitoring dashboard

Acceptance Criteria:

* UI represents relationships and flow between systems and data entities

Summary: Data inconsistency across dashboards and source tables
Description:
Acceptance Criteria:

* Dashboards, logs, and data tables reflect aligned and consistent system information

Summary: Denormalized data misalignment with source system records
Description:
Acceptance Criteria:

* Aggregated / derived data correctly reflects underlying normalized data

Acceptance Criteria:

* System retrieves and processes data from simulated external API endpoint

Summary: Relational database schema design for systems, logs, and transactions
Description:
Acceptance Criteria:

* Schema supports relationships between systems, logs, and operational data

Summary: Simulated multi-system datasets (patients, transactions, system events)
Description:
Acceptance Criteria:

* Database populated with structured datasets representing multiple systems and events

Summary: System monitoring dashboards displaying system health and KPIs
Description:
Acceptance Criteria:

* Dashboard displays system status, KPIs, and integration health indicators

Summary: Integration status tracking across simulated systems
Description:
Acceptance Criteria:

* Dashboards reflect real-time status of integrations (success, error, pending)
